moodboard_atmospheres.teal: System-alive · search active · intelligence present · rgba(90,171,204,…)

carousels.hook: The notebook is the most accurate document on the vessel.
carousels.atmosphere: amber on slide 3 (cost) → green on slide 7 (verified record)
carousels.why_engages: Universally-recognised artifact. Every chief has a notebook or inherited one. Validated at 149 imp under throttle — outlier potential unthrottled + rendered.
carousels.anti_sameness: Physical-private notebook as artifact — no other carousel centres on a personal record.
carousels.caption:
Every vessel has a notebook. A drawer. A diary. Fault codes scratched in margins. Workarounds noted in the back. Suppliers known only by first name.

The notebook is the most accurate document on the vessel. It is also the only one that leaves with the engineer who wrote it.

slides.h: The notebook is the most accurate document on the vessel.
slides.b: A handwritten diary in a drawer. Fault codes. Workarounds. Diagrams the yard never received.

slides.h: It is also the only one that leaves with the engineer who wrote it.
slides.b: The replacement engineer inherits the official records. The notebook is in a kitchen drawer in another country.

slides.h: Three years of accumulated knowledge.
slides.b: Inherited by the next chief as PDFs and silence. The notebook never made it past the gangway.

slides.h: The PMS is the public face of the vessel.
slides.b: The notebook is where the knowledge lives.

slides.h: Every vessel has data. Few have memory.
The notebook stays.
Indexed. Signed. Searchable from the vessel's record.
The next chief inherits everything the last one knew.
Control over your complexity.
slides.b: celeste7.ai

carousels.atmosphere: teal on slide 6 (the system noticing) → green on slide 8
carousels.why_engages: The 'right part was on the shelf' reveal is the canonical 'wait, you can do that?' moment. Most chiefs have made this exact mistake.
carousels.anti_sameness: Equipment-specific (watermaker), inventory × fault join. First use of teal atmosphere.
carousels.caption:
The port watermaker is degrading. You log it. Move on. Three weeks later you order the wrong membrane. The right one was already aboard — bought during the last refit, logged against a different equipment record.

You did not need to remember. The system did.

slides.h: The port watermaker is degrading.
slides.b: TDS readings slipping. Recovery rate down 12% in six weeks.

slides.h: You log the symptom. The PMS shows the equipment record.
slides.b: You move on.

slides.h: Three weeks later you order the wrong membrane.
slides.b: Wrong size. Lead time eleven days. Charter starts in five.

slides.h: The right membrane was on the shelf the entire time.
slides.b: Bought during the last refit. Logged against a different equipment record.

slides.h: You did not need to remember it. The system knows.
Fault logged: TDS rising.
One click. Pre-populated work order. Inventory deducted. Handover updated.
The system that connects the dots.
Control over your complexity.
slides.b: celeste7.ai

carousels.hook: The yard invoice said stabiliser system serviced.
carousels.atmosphere: red on slide 4 (denied claim) → green on slide 7 (signed WO)
carousels.why_engages: €20-100k denied-claim number is a cost-bomb every OWN reads. Combines validated invoice-not-record framing with denied-warranty thread.
carousels.anti_sameness: Stabiliser equipment (varies from generator/main engine). Cost-bomb anchored.
carousels.caption:
The yard invoice said stabiliser system serviced. The hydraulic fluid was never changed. Twelve months later the stabiliser failed under warranty. The claim was denied.

A denied warranty claim on a main engine or generator runs between €20,000 and €100,000. The maintenance is usually done. The proof is the issue.

An invoice is not a record. A signed work order is.

slides.h: The yard invoice said stabiliser system serviced.
slides.b: The hydraulic fluid was never changed.

slides.h: The invoice was filed. The work was assumed.
slides.b: The vessel paid. The crew moved on.

slides.h: Twelve months later the stabiliser failed.
slides.b: Warranty claim denied — no proof of fluid change.

slides.h: €20,000 to €100,000 per denied claim.
slides.b: The maintenance is usually done. The proof is the issue.

slides.h: An invoice is not a record. A signed work order is.
The invoice. The work order. The signature.
Linked. Attributed. Defensible.
The proof lives where the work was done.
Control over your complexity.
slides.b: celeste7.ai

carousels.bank_ref: New · derived from B.4 MLC compliance
carousels.hook: Fourteen crew. Three weeks of hours-of-rest forms. One highlighter.
carousels.atmosphere: amber on slide 3 (manual audit) → green on slide 7
carousels.why_engages: New scenario every CO has done. Targets CO — under-covered stakeholder. Compliance audience expands ICP. Hook is the artifact list — the Saturday morning is inferred, not narrated (§3.3 projection-ban fix 2026-05-15).
carousels.anti_sameness: Compliance / MLC angle. Targets CO. Negative-space query is the unique mechanism.
carousels.caption:
Fourteen crew. Three weeks of hours-of-rest forms. One highlighter. The compliance audit, done by hand at the helm desk.

Every PMS shows what was signed. None of them show who has not. The gap takes forty minutes to find by hand.

Or it announces itself.

slides.h: Fourteen crew. Three weeks of hours-of-rest forms. One highlighter.
slides.b: The compliance audit. Done by hand, at the helm desk.

slides.h: The chief officer checks each row by hand.
slides.b: Green ticks. Red question marks. Forty minutes to find what is missing.

slides.h: Every PMS shows what was signed.
slides.b: None of them show who has not.

slides.h: By Monday the inspection has arrived.
slides.b: The gap is whatever the highlighter missed.

slides.h: The records do not need to be reached. They need to announce themselves.
Who hasn't signed?
The named gap. Surfaced before the inspection.
The unsigned. The names. The dates. One query.
Control over your complexity.
slides.b: celeste7.ai

carousels.atmosphere: amber on slide 3 → green on slide 7
carousels.why_engages: 45-minute number universally recognised. Time-as-artifact framing is novel.
carousels.anti_sameness: Time as primary subject (no other carousel). Pairs with P-001 handover but different angle.
carousels.caption:
Forty-five minutes is what the outgoing chief is given. Two years aboard. Forty-five minutes to transfer it.

The relief nods, takes notes, asks the questions he can think to ask. He will discover the questions he should have asked over the next six months. Every time something breaks.

Forty-five minutes determines two years.

slides.h: Forty-five minutes.
slides.b: The time the outgoing chief has been given to brief the relief.

slides.h: Two years aboard. Forty-five minutes to transfer it.
slides.b: Fault patterns. Supplier relationships. Workarounds never written down because there was no time to write them down.

slides.h: The relief nods. Takes notes. Asks the questions he can think to ask.
slides.b: He will discover the questions he should have asked over the next six months. Every time something breaks.

slides.h: Forty-five minutes determines two years.
The handover should not depend on what one chief can remember to say in forty-five minutes of time.
The handover that wrote itself.
Eight months of context. Signed. Indexed.
Forty-five minutes is now enough.
Control over your complexity.
slides.b: celeste7.ai

carousels.hook: The cooling pump sat on the shelf for nineteen days.
carousels.atmosphere: amber on slide 3 (duplicate order) → teal on slide 6 (system notices)
carousels.why_engages: Every CE has duplicate-ordered. PURSER (procurement) stakeholder rare in slate. '19 days on the shelf then two years' specificity reads as observed. Slide 1 is an artifact-fact, no second-person 'you' (§3.3 projection-ban fix 2026-05-15).
carousels.anti_sameness: Procurement angle (different from watermaker inventory-fault join — that one is fault-side, this is procurement-side). PURSER rare.
carousels.caption:
The cooling pump was ordered twice. Different chief. Different fault. Same part number.

The first one sat on the shelf for nineteen days. Then in stores for two more years.

Every refit. Every rotation. The stock was already aboard.

slides.h: The cooling pump sat on the shelf for nineteen days.
slides.b: Then in stores for two more years.

slides.h: It had been ordered twice.
slides.b: Different chief. Different fault. Same part number.

slides.h: Three-day lead time on the reorder.
slides.b: The first one was already aboard.

slides.h: Every refit. Every rotation. The stock was already aboard.
The part was never missing. The link to it was.
Fault: low cooling pressure.
One click. The pump arrives from the shelf, not the supplier.
The stock that was always there.
Control over your complexity.
slides.b: celeste7.ai

carousels.atmosphere: red on slide 3 (deactivation) → green on slide 7
carousels.why_engages: Combines universal email-loss with compliance angle. Already 172 imp; relevant to CAPT/OWN audiences who don't engage with engineer content.
carousels.anti_sameness: Email as artifact (different from physical Notebook in W2-1 and Word doc in P-001).
carousels.caption:
The contractor sent the fire suppression certificate by email. The email landed in the captain's inbox.

The captain left six months later. The inbox was deactivated within a week.

The vessel paid for the certificate and lost it on the same day.

slides.h: The fire suppression certificate.
slides.b: Sent by email from the contractor to the captain. Filed in the inbox.

slides.h: The captain left the vessel six months later.
slides.b: Inbox deactivated within a week. Normal management policy.

slides.h: The certificate still exists on a server in another country.
slides.b: Issued correctly. Paid for. Filed. Functionally lost.

slides.h: Critical records live in inboxes. Inboxes outlive nobody.
The vessel did not own its proof. The departing captain did.
slides.b: Until now.

slides.h: The email is the record.
Indexed. Linked. Retained.
The vessel owns its records. Not the inbox.
Control over your complexity.
slides.b: celeste7.ai

carousels.atmosphere: red on slide 1 (alarm) → teal on slide 6 (matched) → green on slide 8
carousels.why_engages: Repeat-fault is most-cited story in interviews. €1,900/recurrence × 3 = €5,700 wasted. Cross-rotation memory = highest-impact promise.
carousels.anti_sameness: Repeat-fault pattern (different from watermaker which is inventory-side). Hard cost numbers anchor.
carousels.caption:
The starboard generator alarmed three times in eighteen months. Three different chiefs. Three first-time diagnoses. €1,900 per recurrence in external technician time.

The fix was in the system the whole time. Filed in someone else's rotation. Unreachable by the chief on duty.

The same diagnostic path is not walked twice.

slides.h: The starboard generator high-temp alarm.
slides.b: Three times in eighteen months.

slides.h: Three different chiefs. Three first-time diagnoses.
slides.b: Each one began the same path. Each one rediscovered the heat exchanger.

slides.h: Two days of external technician time. Again.
€950 per day. €1,900 per recurrence.
slides.b: The fix was in the system the whole time.

slides.h: Same fault to the equipment. Three separate faults to the system.
This alarm has occurred before.
The third chief reads what the first chief found.
The diagnostic path is not walked twice.
Control over your complexity.
slides.b: celeste7.ai

carousels.hook: The safety equipment certificate expired in port.
carousels.atmosphere: amber on slide 3 (lapsed cert) → green on slide 7
carousels.why_engages: Date-math against personnel schedule is a 'wait, you can do that?' moment. CAPT under-covered. €4,200 in late fees real number.
carousels.anti_sameness: Certificate-management angle different from P-040 inspection-day. Date-arithmetic novelty.
carousels.caption:
The safety equipment certificate expired in port. The captain who knew was on shore leave. The captain aboard had not been told to look.

Twelve certificates expire each year. Two captains rotate through each calendar. The gap is ashore if nobody owns the bridge.

Cert × calendar × rotation — three systems, one decision.

slides.h: The safety equipment certificate expired in port.
slides.b: The captain was at sea when it lapsed.

slides.h: The renewal was scheduled. The captain who knew was on shore leave.
slides.b: The captain aboard had not been told to look.

slides.h: Two flag-state notices later, the vessel discovered the gap.
slides.b: €4,200 in late fees. Three weeks of correspondence.

slides.h: Twelve certificates expire each year. Two captains rotate through each calendar.
slides.b: The gap is ashore if nobody owns the bridge.

slides.h: Cert × calendar × rotation — three systems, one decision.
Which certificates expire while the captain is on rotation?
The lapsing certificate, with the renewal contact already attached.
The bridge does not depend on which captain reads the diary.
Control over your complexity.
slides.b: celeste7.ai

carousels.hook: The captain forwarded the supplier's note about the gyro.
carousels.atmosphere: red on slide 3 (lost record) → green on slide 7
carousels.why_engages: CAPT-anchored (under-covered). Email-as-knowledge fits /handover claim. Gyro varies equipment surface.
carousels.anti_sameness: Email artifact but different from W2-7 (inbox deactivation). CAPT-anchored.
carousels.caption:
The captain forwarded the supplier's note about the gyro last March. The chief was on rotation. He read it on a Tuesday. He did not reply.

Nine months later the gyro fails. The supplier's note had named the failure mode. The vessel does not have the note.

Correspondence is operational truth. Treat it like one.

slides.h: The captain forwarded the supplier's note about the gyro.
slides.b: Last March. To the chief.

slides.h: The chief was on rotation. He read it on a Tuesday. He did not reply.
slides.b: The thread closed.

slides.h: Nine months later the gyro fails.
slides.b: The supplier's note had named the failure mode. The vessel does not have the note.

slides.h: The captain's email was forwarded. It was never bound to the equipment.
The thread closed. The risk did not.
Email · linked to equipment.
Searchable from the equipment. Surfaced when the fault returns.
Correspondence is operational truth. Treat it like one.
Control over your complexity.
slides.b: celeste7.ai

carousels.hook: The yard delivered the drawing in 2018.
carousels.atmosphere: amber on slide 3 → green on slide 7
carousels.why_engages: Untapped scenario (refit scope-setting). YPM stakeholder rare. Most chiefs have shown a yard the drawing and watched the PM realise it's wrong.
carousels.anti_sameness: Drawing as artifact. Refit / YPM scenario unique in slate.
carousels.caption:
The yard delivered the drawing in 2018. Stamped. Signed. The most authoritative document on the vessel.

Six refits later, the drawing is a fiction. The wiring changed. The valve was rerouted. The starboard pump was moved port. None of it went back to the yard.

The drawing is still the most authoritative document. That is the problem.

slides.h: The yard delivered the drawing in 2018.
slides.b: Stamped. Signed. The most authoritative document on the vessel.

slides.h: Six refits later, the drawing is a fiction.
slides.b: The wiring changed. The valve was rerouted. The starboard pump was moved port. None of it went back to the yard.

slides.h: The next refit begins with a drawing that no longer matches the vessel.
slides.b: The yard quotes from fiction. The crew corrects in person. The cost is the correction.

slides.h: The drawing is still the most authoritative document. That is the problem.
The drawing did not change. The vessel did. Nothing tracked the gap.
Drawing + modifications + change history.
The vessel in 2018. The vessel today. Both signed.
The refit begins from reality, not from 2018.
Control over your complexity.
slides.b: celeste7.ai

carousels.hook: Twelve gigabytes of the engine room. On a phone.
carousels.atmosphere: teal on slide 6 → green on slide 7
carousels.why_engages: Photo-as-artifact under-told. 12 GB of operational photos sit on personal phones. CS audience crossover. Slide 1 names the artifact (photos as data on a phone), no retrospective time-jump narration (§3.3 projection-ban fix 2026-05-15).
carousels.anti_sameness: Image-domain artifact (no other carousel touches photos). Crosses CS audience first time.
carousels.caption:
Twelve gigabytes of the engine room sits on a crew member's phone. Before the refit. Before the controller was replaced.

The most accurate visual record of the vessel — and the only one the next chief will never see.

The crew left. The phone left with them.

slides.h: Twelve gigabytes of the engine room. On a phone.
slides.b: A crew member's camera roll. Before the refit. Before the controller was replaced.

slides.h: The most accurate visual record of the vessel.
slides.b: Unstructured. Personal. Dated only by the file system.

slides.h: And the only one the next chief will never see.
slides.b: It rotated off with the crew member who shot it.

slides.h: The crew left. The phone left with them.
The vessel does not own its picture. The departing crew did.
Photo · linked to equipment.
Searchable from the equipment record. Inherited at rotation.
The vessel keeps its own picture.
Control over your complexity.
slides.b: celeste7.ai

carousels.bank_ref: 02_problem_validation.md:11+:47 · chase-the-predecessor facet of validated handover-class painkiller
carousels.pillar: 3 · Artifact Transform · WhatsApp → CelesteOS search result
carousels.scenario: Six weeks into rotation, predecessor unreachable
carousels.atmosphere: red on slide 4 (cost) → green on slide 9 (reveal)
carousels.why_engages: Every chief has been on either side of this 11pm message. Pain class = knowledge walks off the boat. Facet = after the handover failed.
carousels.anti_sameness: WhatsApp artefact (chase) vs. Word doc artefact (handover). Same pain class, different artefact, post-handover moment.
carousels.caption:
A chief joins a vessel six weeks into a rotation. An alarm pattern he hasn't seen before. The previous engineer would know — he ran this boat for two years. He left a forwarding number that no longer works. His personal mobile is six hours ahead and showing offline.

The vessel does not answer questions. Only people do. And the person who could is on a beach in Thailand with a number that has been deactivated.

The vessel's knowledge, kept where the next person can reach it.

slides.h: Are you awake?
slides.b: Hey mate. Quick one if you're around — port genny alarming high coolant temp underway. Known thing? Sorry to bother.Sent 23:14 · Delivered · No reply

slides.h: The vessel does not answer questions.
slides.b: The handbook does. The PMS does, after twenty minutes of menus. The previous engineer answered in thirty seconds — when he was here.

slides.h: He left six weeks ago.
slides.b: His work mobile was deactivated the day he flew home. His personal number shows a six-hour time delta and offline.

slides.h: The fault eats the day either way.
slides.b: If he replies, the new chief loses the morning waiting. If he doesn't, the new chief loses the week diagnosing from scratch. The vessel pays for both.

slides.h: The vessel knows. The vessel cannot say.
slides.b: Every fault he ever closed. Every workaround he ever filed. Every quirk he ever found. All of it lived in his head. None of it lived where the next person could reach it.

slides.h: The question is asked. Of the system.
Pulled. Cited. Confirmed by the engineer who knows.
The next person does not start over.
Introducing CelesteOS.
slides.b:
The vessel's knowledge, kept where the next person can reach it.

Every answer cites its source. The crew decides.
